name: Chicken and Coconut Milk Soup (Gaeng Com Yam Gai)
recipeIngredient: 1 small Chicken, sectioned and cut into bite-sized pieces (bone-in)
3 Stalks Lemon grass, bruised and cut into 1-inch lengths
recipeInstructions:
1. Juice of 2 limes
2. 3 tablespoons Fish sauce (Nam Pla)
Here's another classic "Tom Yam" type chicken soup. The Laos powder is dried galangal, powdered. Unlike ginger, dried galangal seems to retain most of its character. If you use canned coconut milk, the Thin milk is the more watery liquid in the can. The thick condensed stuff is coconut cream (not to be confused with the syrupy sweet coconut cream used for Pina Coladas). If you shake the can up and combine the two, you have thick coconut milk.
3. A lovely lemony, creamy soup. Chicken pieces should be cut through the bone with a heavy cleaver, Chinese style. If you find gnawing on chicken pieces and delicately trying to remove the bone inhibiting your dinner conversation, you may debone the bird and substitute chicken pieces. Use both dark and light meats for color and nutrition.
4. In a saucepan, bring Thin coconut milk to a boil. Add the chicken pieces, lemon grass, and Laos powder. Reduce heat and simmer until the chicken is tender, about 15 minutes. Do not cover as this will tend to curdle the coconut milk.
5. When the chicken is tender, add the green onions, coriander leaves, and chillies. Bring the heat up just below boiling. Remove the pan from heat, stir in lime juice, fish sauce, and serve.
6. NOTE: Beef cut into thin strips or firm white fish pieces may be substituted for chicken.
From "The Original Thai Cookbook" by Jennifer Brennan; Published by Putnam; 1981.
